Dashboard.title: Calculator Tools
Dashboard.subtitle: Select the calculator you need and start computing immediately. Clean, simple, and fast.
Dashboard.ohmDesc: Standard calculations for V = I × R.
Dashboard.ohmCeoTitle: Ohm's Law Calculator - Free V=IR Calculator Online
Dashboard.ohmCeoDesc: Free Ohm's Law calculator to instantly calculate voltage, current, and resistance. Essential tool for electrical circuits, electronics projects, and educational purposes.
Dashboard.kirchhoffDesc: Circuit analysis with KCL and KVL.
Dashboard.kirchhoffCeoTitle: Kirchhoff's Laws Calculator - KCL & KVL Circuit Analysis
Dashboard.kirchhoffCeoDesc: Analyze complex electrical circuits using Kirchhoff's Current Law (KCL) and Voltage Law (KVL). Calculate node voltages and mesh currents. Professional tool for electrical engineering and circuit design.
Dashboard.powerDesc: Calculate electrical power P = V × I.
Dashboard.powerCeoTitle: AC Power Calculator - Active Reactive Apparent Power
Dashboard.powerCeoDesc: Calculate active power (kW), reactive power (kVAR), and apparent power (kVA) for AC electrical circuits. Power factor and phase angle calculator. Professional tool for electrical engineering and energy systems.
Dashboard.resistorCeoTitle: Resistor Color Code Calculator - 4 5 6 Band Decoder
Dashboard.resistorCeoDesc: Instantly decode resistor color bands. Calculate resistance value, tolerance, and temperature coefficient for 4-band, 5-band, and 6-band resistors. Essential tool for electronics hobbyists, engineers, and students.
Dashboard.bodeCeoTitle: Bode Plot Calculator - Frequency Response Analysis
Dashboard.bodeCeoDesc: Generate Bode magnitude and phase plots for low-pass and high-pass filters. Calculate cutoff frequency and gain in dB. Professional visualization tool for control systems and electronic circuit design.
Dashboard.capacitorChargeCeoTitle: Capacitor Charge Calculator - RC Time Constant
Dashboard.capacitorChargeCeoDesc: Calculate capacitor charge and discharge voltage over time in RC circuits. Analyze time constant (tau) and instantaneous voltage values. Practical tool for electronic circuit design.
Dashboard.baseConverterCeoTitle: Base Converter - Binary to Hex to Decimal Converter
Dashboard.baseConverterCeoDesc: Instantly convert between binary, decimal, hexadecimal, and octal number systems. Free conversion tool for programming, computer science, and digital electronics.
Dashboard.cronParserCeoTitle: Cron Parser - Cron Expression Decoder & Visualizer
Dashboard.cronParserCeoDesc: Decode and visualize cron expressions into readable English. Parse Linux cron job schedules with minute, hour, day, month, and weekday values. Essential tool for system administrators, developers, and DevOps engineers.
Dashboard.jsonFormatterCeoTitle: JSON Formatter - Online JSON Validator & Prettifier
Dashboard.jsonFormatterCeoDesc: Format, validate, and visualize your JSON data instantly. Detect syntax errors, minify and prettify JSON. Essential tool for API development, web programming, and data analysis. Free online JSON beautifier.
Dashboard.compoundInterestCeoTitle: Compound Interest Calculator - Investment Growth Calculator
Dashboard.compoundInterestCeoDesc: Calculate the future value of your investments with compound interest. Includes inflation impact, monthly/yearly contributions, and capital growth analysis. Retirement and long-term investment planning tool.
Dashboard.cryptoPnlCeoTitle: Crypto P&L Calculator - Bitcoin Profit Loss Tracker
Dashboard.cryptoPnlCeoDesc: Calculate profit and loss for Bitcoin, Ethereum, and altcoin trades instantly. Average cost basis, ROI calculator, and portfolio tracking. Professional P&L tool for crypto investors and traders.
Dashboard.concreteSectionCeoTitle: Concrete Section Design - Reinforced Concrete Beam Analysis
Dashboard.concreteSectionCeoDesc: Perform section design and strength analysis for reinforced concrete beams. Calculate reinforcement area, concrete grade, and moment capacity. Professional tool for civil engineers and structural designers.
Dashboard.soilMechanicsCeoTitle: Soil Mechanics Calculator - Bearing Capacity & Strength
Dashboard.soilMechanicsCeoDesc: Calculate soil shear strength using the Mohr-Coulomb criterion. Analyze cohesion, internal friction angle, and normal stress. Free tool for geotechnical engineering and foundation design.
Dashboard.beamCeoTitle: Beam Deflection Analysis - Structural Strength Calculator
Dashboard.beamCeoDesc: Calculate maximum deflection and bending for simply supported and cantilever beams. Analyze elastic modulus and moment of inertia. Professional tool for mechanical and civil engineering strength calculations.
Dashboard.stressStrainCeoDesc: Calculate stress, strain, and Young's modulus for materials. Analyze force, area, and change in length. Ideal tool for material science and strength of materials courses.
Dashboard.shearMomentCeoTitle: Shear & Moment Diagram Plotter - BMD & SFD Solver
Dashboard.shearMomentCeoDesc: Visualize shear force (SFD) and bending moment (BMD) diagrams for beams. Calculate support reactions and maximum moment points instantly.
Dashboard.springConstantCeoTitle: Spring Constant Calculator - Hooke's Law Solver
Dashboard.springConstantCeoDesc: Calculate the relationship between force, spring constant, and displacement using Hooke's Law. Practical spring calculator for mechanical design and physics problems.
Dashboard.periodicTableCeoDesc: Explore all 118 chemical elements with atomic mass, electron configuration, melting point, and discovery information. Perfect for students and chemists.
Dashboard.idealGasCeoTitle: Ideal Gas Law Calculator - PV=nRT Solver
Dashboard.idealGasCeoDesc: Calculate the relationship between pressure, volume, amount of substance, and temperature using the Ideal Gas Equation. Fast and precise gas law solver for chemistry and thermodynamics.
Dashboard.molarityCeoDesc: Calculate molarity, moles, and solution volume instantly. Precise concentration solver for chemistry labs and academic research.
Dashboard.bernoulliCeoDesc: Analyze energy conservation in ideal fluids using Bernoulli's equation. Calculate transitions between pressure, velocity, and height. Tool for fluid mechanics courses and hydraulic calculations.
Dashboard.pressureLossCeoDesc: Calculate friction-induced pressure drop and head loss in pipelines. Analyze friction factor, pipe diameter, and flow velocity. Professional tool for plumbing and hydraulic system design.
Dashboard.normalCeoDesc: Generate normal distribution (bell curve) charts with mean and standard deviation. Analyze probability density function and empirical rules. Visualization tool for statistics and data analysis.
Dashboard.basicStatsCeoTitle: Basic Statistics Calculator - Mean Median Mode
Dashboard.basicStatsCeoDesc: Instantly calculate mean, median, mode, standard deviation, and variance for your data sets. Sample and population analysis. Comprehensive tool for data science and statistics students.
Dashboard.discreteDistCeoDesc: Calculate and visualize Binomial and Poisson distributions. Analyze expected value and variance. Professional tool for probability calculations and statistical modeling.
Dashboard.dataVizCeoDesc: Transform your raw data into histogram charts and examine distribution summaries. Group data sets and visualize frequency distributions. Practical tool for data analysis.
Dashboard.calculusCeoDesc: Calculate derivatives and integrals of functions instantly. Compute limits, critical points, and area calculations. Online calculus tool for engineering and mathematics students.
Dashboard.matrixCeoTitle: Matrix Calculator - Determinant & Inverse Matrix Solver
Dashboard.matrixCeoDesc: Perform matrix addition, multiplication, determinant, and inverse matrix calculations. Professional calculator for 2x2, 3x3, and larger matrices. Ideal tool for linear algebra students and engineers.
Dashboard.geometryCeoDesc: Calculate area, perimeter, and volume for geometric shapes including squares, rectangles, circles, triangles, prisms, cylinders, and spheres. Comprehensive tool for geometry students and engineers.
Dashboard.functionPlotCeoDesc: Plot and analyze mathematical functions on a coordinate system. Visualize roots, intercepts, and changes. Interactive graphing tool for math education and analysis.
Dashboard.quadraticSolverCeoDesc: Calculate roots (x1, x2) and discriminant of quadratic equations instantly. Fast solving tool for mathematics and engineering problems.

converters.unitConverterCeoDesc: Convert units across 12+ categories including length, mass, temperature, pressure, and energy. Precise and comprehensive unit converter for engineering and scientific calculations.

ohm.content:
Ohm's Law defines the relationship between voltage (V), current (I), and resistance (R) in an electrical circuit. The fundamental formula is V = I × R.

• **Voltage (V):** The electrical potential difference between two points (Unit: Volt).
• **Current (I):** The flow rate of electric charge (Unit: Ampere).
• **Resistance (R):** The opposition to current flow (Unit: Ohm).

kirchhoff.content:
Kirchhoff's Laws are the foundation for analyzing complex circuits:

1. **Current Law (KCL):** The total current entering a junction equals the total current leaving.
2. **Voltage Law (KVL):** The sum of all voltage differences around a closed loop is zero.

power.content:
Electrical power measures the rate at which energy is transferred by an electrical circuit. For Direct Current (DC), P = V × I is used, while Alternating Current (AC) introduces apparent, active, and reactive power concepts.

• **Active Power (P):** The real power that performs work (Unit: Watt).
• **Apparent Power (S):** The total power supplied to the system (Unit: VA).
• **Power Factor (cosφ):** An indicator of energy efficiency.

resistor.content:
Resistor color codes are used to quickly identify a resistor's value and tolerance.

• **First Bands:** Represent the significant digits.
• **Multiplier Band:** Indicates the multiplier value.
• **Tolerance Band:** Shows the possible deviation from the nominal value (e.g., 5%, 10%).

bode.content:
A Bode Plot is used to visualize a system's frequency response (magnitude and phase). It is essential for analyzing the performance of electronic filters and control systems.

• **Magnitude Plot:** Shows the gain (dB) relative to frequency.
• **Phase Plot:** Shows the phase difference (degrees) between input and output signals.

baseConverter.content:
The Number Systems Converter allows numbers to be translated between different mathematical bases (binary, decimal, hexadecimal, etc.). It is fundamental for understanding physical representations of data in computer architecture.

• **Binary (2):** The fundamental language of computers, consisting of 0s and 1s.
• **Hexadecimal (16):** Commonly used for memory addresses and color codes.
• **Decimal (10):** The base-10 system used in everyday life.

cronParser.content:
The Cron Expression Parser decodes complex scheduling strings (e.g., */15 * * * *) used for periodic tasks in Unix-based systems.

• **Asterisk (*):** Represents every value.
• **Question Mark (?):** Indicates no specific value.
• **Slash (/):** Specifies step values.

jsonFormatter.content:
The JSON Formatter structures JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) data for better readability or compresses it for faster network transmission.

• **Format (Beautify):** Adds indentation to make the data human-readable.
• **Minify:** Removes all whitespace to reduce file size.
• **Validation:** Detects syntax errors.

compoundInterest.content:
The Compound Interest Calculator works on the principle that the interest added to the principal also earns interest in subsequent periods. It is a fundamental financial tool for understanding the power of long-term investments.

• **Principal:** The initial amount invested.
• **Compound Frequency:** How often the interest is calculated (monthly, yearly, etc.).
• **Time:** How long the investment remains in the system.

cryptoPnl.content:
The Crypto P&L Calculator analyzes potential or realized profit/loss in your cryptocurrency trades. It is critical for understanding risk management in leveraged positions.

• **ROI (Return on Investment):** The efficiency of an investment relative to its cost.
• **PnL (Profit and Loss):** The net cash change after the trade.
• **Leverage:** The ability to open larger positions with a smaller amount of capital.

ElementDescriptions.H: The lightest and most abundant element in the universe.
ElementDescriptions.He: Noble gas used in balloons and cooling systems.
ElementDescriptions.Li: Lightest metal used in batteries and psychiatric medications.
ElementDescriptions.Be: Steel-gray metal used in aerospace and X-ray windows.
ElementDescriptions.B: Essential for plant growth, used in borosilicate glass.
ElementDescriptions.C: The basis of all known life, exists as diamond and graphite.
ElementDescriptions.N: Makes up 78% of Earth's atmosphere, essential for life.
ElementDescriptions.O: Essential for respiration, makes up 21% of atmosphere.
ElementDescriptions.F: Most reactive element used in toothpaste and Teflon production.
ElementDescriptions.Ne: Noble gas used in advertising signs and high voltage indicators.
ElementDescriptions.Na: Highly reactive metal essential for nerve function.
ElementDescriptions.Mg: Light structural metal that burns with bright white flame.
ElementDescriptions.Al: Most abundant metal in Earth's crust, lightweight and recyclable.
ElementDescriptions.Si: Foundation of modern electronics and semiconductor industry.
ElementDescriptions.P: Essential for DNA and ATP, glows in the dark.
ElementDescriptions.S: Yellow solid essential for proteins, used in fertilizer production.
ElementDescriptions.Cl: Toxic gas used in water treatment and bleach production.
ElementDescriptions.Ar: Third most abundant gas in atmosphere, used in welding.
ElementDescriptions.K: Highly reactive metal essential for nerve and muscle function.
ElementDescriptions.Ca: Essential for bones and teeth, most abundant metal in body.
ElementDescriptions.Sc: Rare earth metal used in aerospace alloys.
ElementDescriptions.Ti: Strong, lightweight, corrosion-resistant metal used in aircraft.
ElementDescriptions.V: Used in steel alloys and redox batteries.
ElementDescriptions.Cr: Used in stainless steel and chrome plating.
ElementDescriptions.Mn: Essential for steel production and battery cathodes.
ElementDescriptions.Fe: Most commonly used metal, essential for hemoglobin.
ElementDescriptions.Co: Used in magnets, batteries, and blue pigments.
ElementDescriptions.Ni: Used in stainless steel and batteries, allergenic.
ElementDescriptions.Cu: Excellent conductor used in electrical cables and electronics.
ElementDescriptions.Zn: Used in galvanization and alloys like brass.
ElementDescriptions.Ga: Melts in your hand, used in LEDs and semiconductors.
ElementDescriptions.Ge: Early semiconductor material used in fiber optics.
ElementDescriptions.As: Toxic element used in semiconductors and pesticides.
ElementDescriptions.Br: Only liquid nonmetal at room temperature, reddish-brown.
ElementDescriptions.Kr: Used in high-performance light bulbs and lasers.
ElementDescriptions.Rb: Highly reactive metal used in atomic clocks and research.
ElementDescriptions.Sr: Used in fireworks and CRT screens, burns with red flame.
ElementDescriptions.Y: Used in LEDs, phosphors, and superconductors.
ElementDescriptions.Zr: Corrosion-resistant metal used in nuclear reactors.
ElementDescriptions.Nb: Used in superconducting magnets and steel alloys.
ElementDescriptions.Mo: High melting point metal used in steel and catalysts.
ElementDescriptions.Tc: First artificially produced element, used in medical imaging.
ElementDescriptions.Ru: Rare metal used in electronics and catalysts.
ElementDescriptions.Rh: Precious metal used in catalytic converters and jewelry.
ElementDescriptions.Pd: Hydrogen-absorbing metal used in catalytic converters.
ElementDescriptions.Ag: Best electrical conductor, used in jewelry and electronics.
ElementDescriptions.Cd: Toxic metal used in batteries and pigments.
ElementDescriptions.In: Soft metal used in touch screens and semiconductors.
ElementDescriptions.Sn: Used in solders, alloys, and tin plating.
ElementDescriptions.Sb: Brittle metalloid used in flame-retardant materials and alloys.
ElementDescriptions.Te: Rare metalloid used in solar panels and thermoelectrics.
ElementDescriptions.I: Essential for thyroid function, violet vapor element.
ElementDescriptions.Xe: Used in high-intensity lamps and ion propulsion systems.
ElementDescriptions.Cs: Most reactive natural element, used in atomic clocks.
ElementDescriptions.Ba: Used in medical imaging and fireworks.
ElementDescriptions.La: First lanthanide used in camera lenses and batteries.
ElementDescriptions.Ce: Most abundant rare earth used in catalytic converters.
ElementDescriptions.Pr: Used in high-strength alloys and yellow glass.
ElementDescriptions.Nd: Produces powerful magnets used in headphones and motors.
ElementDescriptions.Pm: Only radioactive lanthanide, used in nuclear batteries.
ElementDescriptions.Sm: Used in magnets and cancer treatment.
ElementDescriptions.Eu: Makes red phosphors for TV and LED screens.
ElementDescriptions.Gd: Used in MRI contrast agents and alloys.
ElementDescriptions.Tb: Used in green phosphors and solid-state devices.
ElementDescriptions.Dy: Used in high-performance magnets and nuclear reactors.
ElementDescriptions.Ho: Has the highest magnetic strength of all elements.
ElementDescriptions.Er: Used in fiber optics and pink glass/tiles.
ElementDescriptions.Tm: Rarest stable lanthanide, used in lasers and X-rays.
ElementDescriptions.Yb: Used in atomic clocks and steel strengthening.
ElementDescriptions.Lu: Densest and hardest lanthanide, used in PET scans.
ElementDescriptions.Hf: Used in nuclear control rods and filaments.
ElementDescriptions.Ta: Corrosion-resistant metal used in electronics and implants.
ElementDescriptions.W: Highest melting point metal, used in light bulbs.
ElementDescriptions.Re: One of the rarest elements, used in jet engines.
ElementDescriptions.Os: Densest naturally occurring element, used in alloys.
ElementDescriptions.Ir: Corrosion-resistant metal used in spark plugs and crucibles.
ElementDescriptions.Pt: Precious metal used in catalytic converters and jewelry.
ElementDescriptions.Au: Excellent conductor, precious metal symbol of wealth.
ElementDescriptions.Hg: Only liquid metal at room temperature, toxic.
ElementDescriptions.Tl: Toxic metal used in electronics and glass.
ElementDescriptions.Pb: Heavy toxic metal used in batteries and radiation shielding.
ElementDescriptions.Bi: Low toxicity metal used in medicine and cosmetics.
ElementDescriptions.Po: Radioactive, used in static electricity eliminators.
ElementDescriptions.At: Shortest-lived natural element, rare radioactive halogen.
ElementDescriptions.Rn: Radioactive noble gas, health risk in basements.
ElementDescriptions.Fr: Extremely rare and radioactive, highly unstable.
ElementDescriptions.Ra: Radioactive, used in luminous paints, now banned.
ElementDescriptions.Ac: First actinide, blue-glowing, used in radiation therapy.
ElementDescriptions.Th: Potential nuclear fuel, more abundant than uranium.
ElementDescriptions.U: Primary nuclear fuel, used in reactors and weapons.
ElementDescriptions.Np: First transuranic element, used in neutron detection.
ElementDescriptions.Pu: Radioactive metal used in nuclear reactors and weapons.
ElementDescriptions.Am: Artificially produced element used in smoke detectors.
ElementDescriptions.Bk: Highly radioactive, used in scientific research.
ElementDescriptions.Cf: Highly radioactive metal used as neutron source.
ElementDescriptions.Es: Extremely radioactive element used in scientific research.
ElementDescriptions.Fm: Radioactive metal produced from explosion inventory.
ElementDescriptions.Md: Radioactive element named after Dmitri Mendeleev.
ElementDescriptions.No: Radioactive actinide named after Alfred Nobel.
ElementDescriptions.Lr: Radioactive element named after Ernest Lawrence.
ElementDescriptions.Rf: Radioactive element named after Ernest Rutherford.
ElementDescriptions.Db: Radioactive element named after Dubna, Russia.
ElementDescriptions.Sg: Radioactive element named after Glenn Seaborg.
ElementDescriptions.Bh: Radioactive element named after Niels Bohr.
ElementDescriptions.Hs: Radioactive element named after the German state of Hesse.
ElementDescriptions.Mt: Radioactive element named after Lise Meitner.
ElementDescriptions.Ds: Radioactive element named after Darmstadt, Germany.
ElementDescriptions.Rg: Radioactive element named after Wilhelm Röntgen.
ElementDescriptions.Cn: Radioactive element named after Nicolaus Copernicus.
ElementDescriptions.Fl: Radioactive element named after Georgy Flerov.
ElementDescriptions.Lv: Radioactive element named after Lawrence Livermore Lab.
ElementDescriptions.Ts: Radioactive element named after the state of Tennessee.
ElementDescriptions.Og: Radioactive noble gas named after Yuri Oganessian.
